title: Stellar Wave 5 — Audit trail for multisig (additive signer evidence)
goal: Extend the persisted Compass audit trail so the multisig story is provable from the record alone. Add OPTIONAL, additive fields to the hosted-side AuditEntry (chain, network, sourceAccount, destination, asset, amount, requiredSigners, collectedSigners, threshold, txHash, networkError, lifecycle), and add a mapper that builds the extended metadata from Wave 0's ChainAuditMetadata plus Wave 4's co-signing result. Every new field is optional so existing Solana audit entries validate unchanged; the brain stays untouched.

context.analysisSource: The hosted-side persisted AuditEntry (shared/types/evaluationContracts.ts, ~lines 72-81) is minimal: correlationId, auditRef, toolName, decision, riskLevel, reasons, outcome, occurredAt. It has NO chain, network, source/destination, asset, amount, txHash, and NO multisig fields. The richer back-side AuditEvent (shared/types/executionGatewayContracts.ts, lines 75-93) has chain/network/transactionSignature but is effectively single-chain and has NO signer/threshold fields. Without signer fields the demo CANNOT show 'Compass did not sign -> not executable' from the audit record. This is a demo-evidence gap.

openQuestions.question: Should Wave 5 extend the lean persisted AuditEntry, the rich back-side AuditEvent, or unify the two?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: Extend the lean persisted AuditEntry additively and carry the signer fields there. AuditEntry is the record that is actually persisted and validated (hosted/audit), so the multisig evidence must live where a reviewer reads it. The new fields are all OPTIONAL, so Solana entries that omit them stay valid. Unifying AuditEntry and AuditEvent is a larger refactor with no demo payoff and is deferred.

openQuestions.question: Should the full transaction XDR be persisted in the audit record, or only the semantic facts plus a hash?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: Persist semantic facts (chain, network, accounts, asset, amount, signer counts, threshold, lifecycle) plus an OPTIONAL hash. Do NOT persist raw XDR, raw tx bytes, or any secret material by default — it is large and privacy-sensitive and the audit record's job is provable evidence, not replay. Full-XDR persistence behind an explicit flag is deferred to a later wave.

acceptanceCriteria: All new fields on AuditEntry are OPTIONAL and additive; an entry with none of them still validates.
Pre-Wave-5 Solana audit entries validate, persist, and list unchanged (backward compatibility).
chain (Wave 0 ChainId) and network are recorded for Stellar audit entries.
requiredSigners, collectedSigners, and threshold are recorded for multisig actions.
sourceAccount, destination, asset, and amount are recorded as semantic facts (no raw XDR).
txHash is recorded on successful execution; networkError is recorded on submission failure.
A lifecycle state from PROPOSED | COSIGNED_BY_COMPASS | SUBMITTED | CONFIRMED | REJECTED | DENIED is recorded.
A mapper builds the extended metadata from Wave 0 ChainAuditMetadata plus the Wave 4 co-signing result.
hosted/audit/auditValidators.ts accepts both the old and the new entry shapes.
The brain (policy engine, LLM judge, sanitizer, COMPASS_DECISIONS, MCP proxy) is untouched and existing Solana tests stay green.
nonGoals: No change to Solana runtime behavior — Solana audit entries keep validating and persisting unchanged.
No change to the brain (policy engine, LLM judge, sanitizer, COMPASS_DECISIONS, MCP proxy).
No persistence of raw XDR, raw tx bytes, private keys, or secret material in the audit record.
No making any new field required.
No new audit storage backend, retention policy, or query API beyond the additive fields.
No frontend/dashboard surfacing of the new fields.
No unification of AuditEntry and AuditEvent.

tasks.title: Add RED tests for additive AuditEntry fields and validator tolerance
tasks.description: Before implementation, write tests asserting: an entry with only the original fields validates (backward compatibility); an entry with the full set of new optional fields validates; an entry with an invalid present field (bad lifecycle, negative threshold, non-ChainId chain) fails validation; a pre-Wave-5 Solana entry round-trips through auditStore write/list unchanged.
testsSpec: isAuditEntry returns true for an entry with only correlationId/auditRef/toolName/decision/riskLevel/reasons/occurredAt
isAuditEntry returns true for an entry that also carries chain/network/requiredSigners/collectedSigners/threshold/lifecycle/txHash
isAuditEntry returns false for lifecycle not in AUDIT_LIFECYCLE_STATES
isAuditEntry returns false for a negative threshold or non-integer collectedSigners
isAuditEntry returns false for a chain value that is not a valid ChainId
a legacy Solana AuditEntry round-trips through auditStore write/list unchanged
No legacy/ import appears in any touched audit file

tasks.title: Add optional AuditEntry fields and the lifecycle const
tasks.description: Add AUDIT_LIFECYCLE_STATES const + AuditLifecycleState type and the OPTIONAL fields (chain, network, sourceAccount, destination, asset, amount, requiredSigners, collectedSigners, threshold, txHash, networkError, lifecycle) to AuditEntry. Keep types/contracts separate from behavior. Update auditValidators isAuditEntry to keep the original required-field checks and add guarded optional-field checks (pass when absent, validate when present).

tasks.title: Add RED tests for the stellar audit metadata mapper
tasks.description: Write tests for buildStellarAuditMetadata before implementation, covering the deny/non-cosigned case, the cosigned/confirmed case, the submission-failure case, and the no-secret-leak guarantee.
testsSpec: buildStellarAuditMetadata for a non-cosigned DENY yields collectedSigners < requiredSigners, sets threshold, and lifecycle DENIED
buildStellarAuditMetadata for a cosigned confirmed execution yields collectedSigners == requiredSigners, a non-empty txHash, and lifecycle CONFIRMED
buildStellarAuditMetadata for a submission failure sets networkError, leaves txHash absent, and sets lifecycle REJECTED
mapper output contains no raw XDR, raw tx bytes, or secret material
No legacy/ import appears in the mapper file

tasks.title: Implement the stellar audit metadata mapper
tasks.description: Create back/services/stellar/audit/stellarAuditMetadata.ts. buildStellarAuditMetadata reads chain-neutral facts from Wave 0 ChainAuditMetadata and signer facts from the Wave 4 co-signing result, derives the lifecycle state, and returns only the optional AuditEntry extension fields. Never read or emit raw XDR or secret material.

tasks.title: Validate and pause for review
tasks.description: Run the full validation suite and stop for parent review. Confirm backward compatibility (Solana entries unchanged), the brain untouched, and no legacy imports before any later wave consumes the enriched audit record.

implementationEvidence.mode: Additive, backward-compatible audit fields + mapper; brain & Solana untouched
filesEdited: shared/types/evaluationContracts.ts (AUDIT_LIFECYCLE_STATES const + AuditLifecycleState; 12 OPTIONAL additive fields on AuditEntry)
hosted/audit/auditValidators.ts (isAuditEntry: guarded optional checks — pass when absent, type-checked when present)
commands: npm run test:back -> 400 passed (43 files), incl. 15 new Wave 5 tests
npm run test:back -- back/services/stellar/audit hosted/audit -> 15 passed
tsc --noEmit -> only the 1 PRE-EXISTING mcpProxyDispatcher error remains
deviations: All 12 new AuditEntry fields are OPTIONAL: chain (Wave 0 ChainId), network, sourceAccount, destination, asset, amount, requiredSigners, collectedSigners, threshold, txHash, networkError, lifecycle. Legacy Solana entries omit them and validate unchanged (verified via auditStore write/list round-trip).

Lifecycle derived (not free-form): denied -> DENIED; networkError -> REJECTED; txHash -> CONFIRMED; cosigned (no terminal) -> COSIGNED_BY_COMPASS; else PROPOSED. (SUBMITTED reserved for an explicit in-flight signal in Wave 6.)
Mapper carries only semantic facts (+ optional txHash); never raw XDR, tx bytes, or secret material. Full-XDR persistence deferred.
isAuditEntry keeps required-field checks unchanged; new checks are guarded so absent fields pass and present fields are validated (invalid chain/lifecycle/negative counts rejected).
